source: Xbox Store + PlayStation Store + GameStop + Best Buy + gg.deals + EA Help
notes: Madden Points are purchased in-game or via platform stores (Xbox, PlayStation, EA App). Not available as Steam DLC. Pricing is consistent across Xbox, PlayStation, and PC (EA App). EA Play subscribers receive a 10% discount. Madden 26 has been announced but MUT 26 pricing not yet confirmed - expect identical structure based on EA's history of maintaining point tiers across Madden iterations.

purchase_tiers.note: Purchased in-game or via platform digital stores. EA Play members get 10% off. Pricing identical on Xbox, PlayStation, and PC (EA App).
purchase_tiers.value_progression_notes: Bonus structure is implicit - the base rate at 500 points is ~$0.01/point. Higher tiers provide progressively more points per dollar. The 18,500 tier delivers ~18.7% more value per dollar than the 500 tier. EA does not advertise explicit 'bonus points' but the effective discount is built into the tier sizing.

item_pricing.note: Madden Points are spent on packs in the MUT Store. Pack availability rotates frequently with promos. Prices below are representative of common pack types seen in MUT 25.

representative_packs.name: Elite Bundle (10x)
representative_packs.contents: 10 Elite packs with guaranteed high-OVR pulls

regional_pricing.note: No specific UK/EUR regional pricing found for Madden Points via web search. EA typically uses approximate currency parity (e.g. $9.99 USD maps to ~8.99 GBP or ~9.99 EUR) consistent with their FC Points pricing. Madden has a smaller European player base than FIFA/FC so regional pricing data is scarce.

what_currency_buys: Packs containing random player cards of varying OVR ratings
Promotional and limited-time event packs (e.g. Ultimate Legends, Team of the Year)
Bundle packs offering multiple packs at a slight discount vs singles
Madden Points CANNOT be used for: direct player purchases from the Auction House (coins only), Training Points, or non-MUT game modes

dual_currency_system.premium_currency: Madden Points (paid only, purchased with real money)
dual_currency_system.earnable_currency: MUT Coins (earned through gameplay, challenges, selling cards on Auction House)
dual_currency_system.secondary_currency: Training Points (earned by quickselling cards, used for player upgrades and abilities)
dual_currency_system.note: Packs can be purchased with either Madden Points or MUT Coins, but bundles are typically Points-only. Training cannot be purchased directly.

economy_notes: Pack-based gacha system - players buy randomised packs for player cards
EA discloses pack odds per regulatory requirements (see pack probability link in urls_cited)
Dual currency: MUT Coins (earnable) and Madden Points (paid only)
Madden Points never go on sale - EA maintains fixed pricing year-round
EA Play subscription provides 10% discount on Madden Points purchases
Bundle packs (points-only) offer best value but require larger spend commitment
Promotional packs released during NFL season events (playoffs, Super Bowl, draft) with boosted odds
No direct trading between players - Auction House uses coins only
Points pricing structure has been consistent across Madden 23, 24, and 25 iterations
